questions.question: Why is media called 'mass media'?

options.A: Because it reaches a large number of people
options.B: Because it is used only by the government
options.C: Because it is available only in cities
options.D: Because it is used only for entertainment

questions.explanation: Mass media reaches a very large audience at the same time.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of print media?
questions.explanation: Newspapers are part of print media because they are printed publications.

questions.question: Which factor strongly influences what appears in media?
questions.explanation: Media is often influenced by business interests and the need to earn profit.

questions.question: Advertisements are mainly used in media to:
questions.explanation: Advertisements promote products and help companies reach customers.

questions.question: Why do television channels show many advertisements?
questions.explanation: Advertisements are a major source of revenue for television channels.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a form of media?
questions.explanation: A notebook is not used for mass communication.

questions.question: Media plays an important role in a democracy because it:
questions.explanation: Media helps citizens stay informed and participate in democracy.

questions.question: Which of the following is electronic media?
questions.explanation: Television is an electronic medium that broadcasts audio and video.

questions.question: Why do companies advertise their products on media platforms?
questions.explanation: Advertisements help companies inform people and increase sales.

questions.question: Which group mainly owns many media companies?
questions.explanation: Many media houses are owned by large business corporations.

questions.question: What does media often try to increase?
questions.explanation: Higher viewership increases advertising revenue.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes media coverage?
questions.explanation: Media coverage refers to reporting events and issues.

questions.question: Which of these is a common source of income for media companies?
questions.explanation: Advertisements provide a major part of media revenue.

questions.question: What does media help people understand?
questions.explanation: Media provides information about events and issues in society.

questions.question: Which medium spreads information quickly across the world?
questions.explanation: The internet allows information to spread instantly worldwide.

questions.question: Why are attractive advertisements created?

options.B: To encourage people to buy products

questions.explanation: Advertisements are designed to influence consumer behavior.

questions.question: Which media platform mainly uses sound to communicate?
questions.explanation: Radio communicates information primarily through audio.

questions.question: Which of these helps media attract advertisers?
questions.explanation: A larger audience attracts more advertisers.

questions.question: Media can influence people's opinions by:
questions.explanation: The way news is presented can shape public opinion.

questions.question: Which of the following is part of broadcast media?
questions.explanation: Radio broadcasts information to a wide audience.

questions.question: Media helps people stay aware of:
questions.explanation: Media informs people about events happening locally and globally.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes media's influence?
questions.explanation: Media influences how people think about issues.

questions.question: Which factor often decides what news gets shown?
questions.explanation: Economic interests and advertising can influence media content.

questions.question: What is the main goal of many media companies?
questions.explanation: Many media companies operate as businesses aiming to make profit.

questions.question: Which media form combines both sound and visuals?
questions.explanation: Television uses both audio and video to communicate.
